mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in less than 50% of low-rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending corporate earnings declines, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in more than 65% of triggered instances over a 5-year historical backtest, or the signal loses predictive power when VIX exceeds 25.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, which translates into higher corporate revenues and positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in more than 60% of cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance with p greater than 0.10 in out-of-sample regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production index values indicate expanding manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side momentum, generating positive short-term S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no statistical edge with Sharpe ratio below 0.5 during early-cycle phases, or the signal reverses direction when core inflation exceeds 3%.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of a federal funds rate below 0.5% and a declining unemployment rate creates a soft-landing environment that supports equity valuations, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint condition fails to predict positive returns in more than 55% of historical instances, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for term premium spreads.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly GDP growth rate falling below 1.5% signals severe demand contraction and recession risk, which forces equity market repricing and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 60% of sub-1.5% GDP growth quarters over a 10-year sample, or the signal is fully explained by concurrent Fed rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls reflect strengthening economic activity and consumer spending power, which directly drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all rolling 3-year windows, or positive payroll surprises consistently precede negative market returns in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid CPI increases combined with a stagnant federal funds rate compress real yields and trigger policy uncertainty, causing immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal produces positive 5-day returns in more than 65% of triggered instances, or the predictive power disappears when CPI growth is driven solely by volatile energy components.
